judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent incorrectly selected the patient cohort by identifying "BRCA" cases via the presence of "Bladder urothelial carcinoma" in Patient_description (execute_python id function-call-4619623954900151400), leading to a female bladder cancer subset rather than female BRCA (breast) patients and resulting in insufficient counts and the incorrect final answer.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent’s final computation (function-call-9742137164995202102) incorrectly filters both rows and columns of the contingency table by marginal totals (dropping mutation True/False columns as well), deviating from the query’s requirement to exclude only histological categories, leading to an altered table and a chi-square statistic (114.889…) that does not match the ground truth.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent attempted to filter female BRCA patients by querying non-existent columns ("gender", "Gender", "sex") in clinical_info, received column-not-found errors, and then stopped without proceeding to join with mutation data or compute the chi-square.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
By extracting female BRCA cases from the free-text Patient_description (execute_python function-call-31576103651757482) and then building a contingency table that contained only one histological type (execute_python function-call-4492796375679995595), the agent selected an incomplete/incorrect cohort, leading it to (wrongly) conclude the chi-square could not be computed instead of producing the correct statistic.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed the chi-square using an incomplete BRCA cohort because, in execute_python (function-call-13745031614548106399), it extracted ParticipantBarcode from free-text Patient_description with the overly specific regex "patient (TCGA-\w{2}-\w{4})", missing many valid barcodes (e.g., lines using "barcode TCGA-..."), which led to an undercounted contingency table and an incorrect chi-square statistic.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@
